title: Determine Why A Tote Was Sent To The Hospital Station Using The Green Scanner
summary: Use the hospital station stacklight and green scanner to confirm tote presence and determine why a tote was brought to the hospital station.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to identify why a tote was brought to the hospital station and the source indicates the hospital station stacklight and green scanner are used for that purpose.
not_for: Detailed tote handling, removal, or reinsertion procedures not explicitly provided in this source section
Any scanner workflow requiring exact scan target, button sequence, or field interpretation not specified in the source

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe the hospital station stacklight, use the green scanner, and identify the reason the tote was brought to the station.

safety_notes: Use only the source-supported diagnostic actions in this runbook.
Do not perform additional tote handling or system control actions unless directed by a separate source-supported procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Ability to observe the hospital station stacklight

system_preconditions: A tote is indicated as present at the hospital station

visual_references.description: Hospital station stacklight reference used to verify tote/AGV presence indication.
Hospital HMI Operations screen showing status information that may help interpret why the tote is at the station.
Example hospital HMI screen for a bar code mismatch condition referenced by the candidate image note.
Example hospital HMI screen for a no-read bar code condition.
Example hospital HMI screen for a tote failed to empty condition.
Example hospital HMI screen for a sent from bagging station condition.
Hospital station operation artifact showing green scanner use and status-field-driven task context.
Reference screen showing green scanner population of Tote ID and barcode fields.

steps.title: Verify tote presence at the hospital station
steps.purpose: Confirm that a tote is present before attempting to determine why it was brought to the hospital station.
steps.instruction: At the hospital station, observe the stacklight indication to verify that a tote is present.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms from the stacklight that a tote is present at the hospital station.
steps.healthy_condition: The stacklight indication shows a tote/AGV present at the station as described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: The stacklight does not indicate tote presence, is indeterminate, or indicates a fault/error condition instead of normal presence.

actions.label: Observe stacklight
actions.exact_action: Check the stacklight indication to verify that a tote is present.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital station stacklight indication meanings, including flashing yellow for 15 seconds meaning an AGV is present at either position of the station.

stop_or_escalate_if: The stacklight does not indicate a tote/AGV present at the station
The stacklight shows fault or RMS error indications
The indication is indeterminate and the operator cannot confirm tote presence

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: A stacklight indicates a tote is present. An operator uses a scanner (green scanner) to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.
Flashing yellow for 15 seconds means an AGV is present at either position of the station; flashing red means fault; red yellow green means RMS error; solid green means no AGV present at the station.

steps.title: Use the green scanner to determine the tote reason
steps.purpose: Use the source-identified scanner method to determine why the tote was brought to the hospital station.
steps.instruction: Use the green scanner at the hospital station on the tote or station process as documented by the source to determine the reason the tote was brought there.
steps.expected_result: The scanner-supported hospital station process provides the reason the tote was brought to the station.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator is able to use the green scanner and obtain a reason or status associated with the tote at the hospital station.
steps.failure_condition: The source-supported scanner interaction cannot be completed, or no reason is returned for the tote.

actions.label: Scan with green scanner
actions.exact_action: Use the green scanner to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital station operation context showing the tote barcode is scanned using the green scanner and the Tote ID field auto-populates.
Status information fields that tell the station operator why the tote is there, what menu they need to use, and what action they need to perform.
Example mismatch condition screen associated with a tote sent to the hospital station because the scanned bar code did not match the tote ID expected by the WCS.
Example no-read condition screen associated with a tote sent to the hospital station because the bar code could not be scanned.
Example failed-to-empty condition screen where the green scanner is used to scan the tote bar code.
Example sent-from-bagging-station condition screen where the green scanner populates Tote ID and Bar Code fields.
Reference example showing green scanner use to populate Tote ID and Barcode fields.

stop_or_escalate_if: The scanner does not provide a reason for the tote at the hospital station
The operator cannot determine the required scan target or scanner interaction from the source
The displayed reason or status cannot be interpreted from the available source material

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An operator uses a scanner (green scanner) to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.
The status messages tell the station operator why the tote is there, what menu they need to use, and what action they need to perform.
The operator scans the tote barcode using the green scanner, confirms the Tote ID field auto-populates, and performs the required task indicated by the status fields.

steps.title: Read and record the returned reason
steps.purpose: Capture the reason identified by the scanner-supported hospital station process so the tote condition can be understood and acted on by the appropriate procedure.
steps.instruction: Read the reason returned by the scanner-supported hospital station process and record that reason for the tote.
steps.expected_result: The operator has identified and recorded the reason the tote was brought to the hospital station.
steps.healthy_condition: A clear tote reason or status is available and can be recorded.
steps.failure_condition: No clear reason is returned, or the reason cannot be read or recorded from the available source-supported interface or process.

actions.label: Read returned reason or status
actions.exact_action: Read the reason returned by the scanner for the tote being brought to the hospital station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Status fields that explain why the tote is there and what action is needed.
Example of a specific reason category: bar code mismatch.
Example of a specific reason category: no read bar code.
Example of a specific reason category: tote failed to empty.
Example of a specific reason category: sent from bagging station.

stop_or_escalate_if: The scanner or associated station process does not provide a reason
The reason cannot be interpreted from the available status information
The source does not provide enough detail to continue to the next handling action

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An operator uses a scanner (green scanner) to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.
Status messages tell the station operator why the tote is there, what menu they need to use, and what action they need to perform.

success_criteria: The operator confirms tote presence at the hospital station.
The green scanner-supported process yields the reason the tote was brought to the station.
The reason is read and recorded by the operator.
healthy_conditions: The stacklight indicates tote/AGV presence at the hospital station.
The green scanner can be used at the hospital station.
A status or reason is available that explains why the tote is at the station.
failure_conditions: The stacklight does not confirm tote presence.
The scanner does not provide a reason for the tote.
The source does not specify enough scanner interaction detail to complete a more detailed procedure.
The returned reason cannot be interpreted from the available source-supported information.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the scanner does not provide a reason for the tote at the hospital station.
Escalate for SME review because the source does not specify the scanner interaction details, scan target, or displayed fields.
Escalate if the stacklight indicates a fault or RMS error instead of normal presence.
